behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to only the lines that changed in the diff, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: GitHub diffs have specific unified diff formatting with hunks, context lines, and variable structures. A pure in-memory mock with simplified diff strings might miss edge cases in how GitHub structures diffs (multiple hunks, deletions vs. additions, line number handling). Testing against realistic GitHub diff formats or actual API responses is necessary to catch parsing boundary bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create test cases with various GitHub unified diff structures (multi-line additions, deletions, multiple hunks, mixed changes with context). Mock or use realistic GitHub API responses. Verify the parser extracts exactly the changed lines and excludes all context lines, handling edge cases like hunks with only deletions or sparse additions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Posting comments at the correct line requires understanding GitHub's specific line numbering semantics in diff view (old-line vs. new-line, unified diff context handling, position in hunk). Mocking the GitHub API would accept any line numbers, hiding bugs where we compute or pass incorrect line references. Only real HTTP calls to GitHub (or extremely faithful mocks of exact API behavior) can verify comments actually appear at the intended positions.
behaviors.test_description: Post inline comments via actual GitHub API (or test against a GitHub-API-compatible test server) for findings at specific diff positions. Verify each comment appears in the PR diff view at the correct line. Test scenarios: additions in the middle of context, deletions, additions at hunk boundaries, and comments on different hunks in the same file.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Whether the code review agent identifies a specific bug pattern (null-pointer dereference, off-by-one error, missing exception handler, logic error) depends on real LLM behavior. The LLM must actually recognize and extract the defect from the code. Mocking the LLM to always return 'bug identified' would pass the test but hide the actual failure (LLM did not recognize the bug in this specific code). Only a real LLM call reveals whether the agent's detection capability actually works.
behaviors.test_description: Create a pull request with a seeded real bug (e.g., missing null check leading to potential NullPointerException, off-by-one loop error, unhandled exception path). Call the actual code review agent with this PR. Verify the agent identifies at least one legitimate defect directly from the real LLM response, not from a mock or hardcoded output.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted—no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: This tests data consistency logic between two generated outputs (inline comments and summary comment). The failure mode is a deterministic logic bug: the code that generates inline comments and the code that generates the summary become out of sync. Mocking the GitHub API to capture both sets of API calls is sufficient to detect this bug. We don't need real API responses or real LLM behavior—only need to wire the components together and verify the data they generate is consistent.
behaviors.test_description: Mock GitHub API to capture both inline comment POST calls and summary comment POST calls. Run the full review pipeline. Extract the findings mentioned in inline comments and the findings mentioned in the summary. Verify every inline-comment finding is in the summary and every summary finding has a matching inline comment. Test with zero findings, single finding, and multiple findings.

behaviors.description: The full sequence—reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment—completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Testing the end-to-end orchestration 'correctly' on a real PR requires the code review agent to actually analyze realistic code with a real LLM. Using a mocked LLM (even with realistic response structure) would not verify the system works on actual PR content. The phrase 'without manual intervention' implies full automation, including real LLM behavior. The test must confirm the pipeline succeeds when given actual code to review, not synthetic mocked responses.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full code review pipeline on a real pull request (or a test PR with actual code changes and known issues). Verify all steps complete without errors: diff is fetched successfully, code review agent runs and processes the code, inline comments are posted, and a summary comment is created. Must use the real LLM to confirm realistic end-to-end behavior on actual code.
